raw_consumer_statement:
I returned a 65 '' XXXX tv and soundbar to Best Buy. I thought I was done with them. 
Then I started receiving bills from Best Buy for the tv I returned. I kept going to the store where I purchased it and asked them to stop the billing. They wanted XXXX ( full price of tv ). I told them I was not going to give them money for something I no longer had. 
They refused to correct the problem. I made several trips to the store and spoke with the manager, XXXX XXXX. XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX Best Buy ). I continued to get the same response.... they wanted XXXX. Then I got an email from my own bank, XXXX, that if I didn't pay the best buy invoices, it might affect my credit score. I texted the manager. No response. Ultimately, it did affect my credit. My credit score dropped from XXXX down to XXXX. 
I talked to XXXX, all kinds of protection agencies, no help. I had my credit score compromised for a tv that I returned. 
When talking to someone at Best Buy corporate, they told me that I was only refunded XXXX with a difference still left on the card of around XXXX. Why did they short me XXXX? I sent a text to the manager asking why they shorted me XXXX .. no reply. 

I have the return receipt, and screen shots of all the texts to and from XXXX XXXX, the manager. 

This has been very stressful. Best Buy told me that this was a " store issue ''. 
Please help me resolve this issue and restore my credit score back to where it was.
